title: Racosperma tuberculatum
taxonomy: Racosperma tuberculatum, also known as the Mugga Ironwood, is classified within the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae, which is a large and diverse group of legumes. The genus Racosperma is a part of the tribe Podalyrieae. The species Racosperma tuberculatum is native to eastern Australia. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the species.
uses: Racosperma tuberculatum, also known as the prickly acacia, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
